description_long: This is a headless marble statue depicting a draped human figure, likely male, wearing a tunic with a lion skin draped over the left shoulder and arm. The garment is rendered with deep, flowing folds, and the lion's head and paws are visible, suggesting a classical or mythological theme, possibly referencing Hercules. The figure stands upright on a rectangular plinth, with both arms missing and the surface showing signs of age and weathering. The back of the statue is less detailed, with the drapery falling in heavier, less defined folds. The overall coloration is a weathered off-white with some darker discolorations, typical of aged marble.
description: A weathered, headless marble statue of a draped human figure with a lion skin over the shoulder, standing on a rectangular plinth.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed drapery, lion skin over the left shoulder, and the absence of the head and arms.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the lion skin's head and paw details draped over the shoulder and arm.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the less detailed, heavy folds of the garment and the back of the plinth.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the drapery's flow and the bulk of the lion skin, with the figure's side profile.
attribute_subject_matter: draped human figure with lion skin, likely referencing Hercules
attribute_location_context: No visible context; the sculpture is shown isolated on a plain base with no environmental cues.
